repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: joint offline-policy misspecification with a mean-side tube, but not this scalar MSM-interval intersection benchmark

published_prior_art.cite: YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 and ZhangXuTan2025: mean-side MSM/eMSM bounds, not the offline-policy mean-versus-quantile dichotomy

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 flags joint compatibility issues, but does not state this fixed-center offline-policy conditional-quantile-tube endpoint

published_prior_art.cite: HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2026 gives MSM-only sharp off-policy value; DornGuo2023 gives bounded-weight quantile geometry; ZhangXuTan2025deMSM, MastenPoirierRen2025, and Wang2026 do not subsume this fixed-center policy-value tube

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: non-rectangular diagnostic for joint misspecification, but not the quantile-envelope representability criterion

published_prior_art.cite: none; the verified MSM/off-policy/distributional-sensitivity papers do not state this Delta_rect equality-versus-strictness criterion
HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2026 supplies the MSM-only one-step score; Firpo2007, ChernozhukovEtAl2018, and Wang2026 supply close templates, but not this coupled contact-face correction

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:joint-feasible / thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The coupled sharp endpoint is conditional on nonempty intersection of the MSM weight window and the fixed-center quantile tube on policy-relevant arms.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The identification theorem is a sharp bound for the restricted nonempty joint class; incompatible laws yield an empty class, not a finite corrected endpoint.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:regular-contact / conj:eif-one-step
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The EIF and root-n inference claim assumes a unique upper optimizer and stable finite-union contact face rather than deriving optimizer regularity.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The inference conjecture is only for regular-contact laws; optimizer switching and support-edge contact remain directional or conservative-inference regimes.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:eif-one-step / fixed center law
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Q0_a and epsilon are treated as fixed analyst inputs rather than estimated nuisance functions.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The one-step claim is conditional on a known center law and known tube radius; external estimation of the center law would require a different target or an added influence contribution.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a worked two- or three-point reward example computing Qrect_a_plus, Qcpl_a_plus, and Delta_rect from the observed arm law to make the representability obstruction auditable before proof search.
Sharpen the equality condition in Theorem 2 by phrasing it in terms of an MSM mean-maximizing law lying inside the tube, then state when this is equivalent to the Qmsm_a_plus envelope condition.
Add a boundary corollary for Gamma=1 and for epsilon large enough to contain both MSM envelopes, explicitly recovering observed-arm policy value and Hess/Frauen MSM-only endpoints.
For the EIF conjecture, state the directional-differentiability fallback as a separate open-ended question with active-set switching as the named focal object.

recommended_next_step: Advance this field-tier angle to Stage 0 with the accepted caveats bound into the theorem statements.
